text: SEBI mandates all mutual fund schemes to display a Risk-o-meter with 6 levels: Low, Low-to-Moderate, Moderate, Moderately High, High, and Very High, updated monthly based on portfolio composition.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: Risk-o-meter – Circular on Product Labeling in Mutual Fund schemes, SEBI Oct 2020

text: ELSS has a mandatory lock-in period of 3 years, which is the shortest among all other tax saving options under Section 80C.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: Has lock-in period of 3 years (which is shortest amongst all other tax saving options)

text: Total Expense Ratio (TER) represents all costs for running and managing a mutual fund scheme, expressed as a percentage of the scheme's average daily NAV.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: All such costs for running and managing a mutual fund scheme are collectively referred to as 'Total Expense Ratio' (TER)

text: SEBI prescribes maximum TER limits: for the first ₹500 crores of AUM in an equity scheme, the maximum TER is 2.25%.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: On the first Rs. 500 crores: 2.25% for Equity funds

text: TER includes management fees, administrative costs, audit fees, registrar fees, custodian fees, and sales & marketing expenses.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: investment management fees, registrar fees, custodian fees, audit fees, sales & marketing expenses

text: The repurchase/redemption price includes the deduction of exit load (if applicable) from the NAV. Entry loads have been abolished by SEBI since 2009.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: The Repurchase/Redemption Price is the price per Unit at which a Mutual Fund would 'repurchase' the units ... and includes Exit Load

text: Redemption price is calculated as: Applicable NAV × (1 - Exit Load). In open-ended schemes, repurchase price shall not be lower than 95% of NAV.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: Redemption Price = Applicable NAV*(1- Exit Load, if any)

text: SEBI Circular dated October 22, 2018 mandates revised TER slabs and performance disclosure norms for mutual funds.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: Total Expense Ratio (TER) and Performance Disclosure for Mutual Funds

text: SEBI Circular dated October 5, 2020 mandates product labeling via Risk-o-meter for all mutual fund schemes to indicate the level of risk.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: Circular on Product Labeling in Mutual Fund schemes – Risk-o-meter

text: To download a capital gains statement from CAMS visit camsonline.com, select Consolidated Account Statement, enter your PAN and registered email address, select the financial year, and the statement is delivered to your registered email.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: Request your consolidated account statement online with CAMS

text: The CAMS Consolidated Account Statement covers all mutual fund transactions across AMCs serviced by CAMS and can be used for capital gains calculation and tax filing.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: Get updated mutual fund account statements easily and securely

text: Investors can download their mutual fund account statement or capital gains report online through CAMS without visiting a branch — only PAN and registered email address are required.

metadata.verbatim_snippet: Request your consolidated account statement online with CAMS
